Mission/Summary: The Alliance for Education supports excellence in public education by advancing educational justice and racial equity for students in Seattle Public Schools. Since 1995, they develop innovative programs, invest in systemic change, and partner with the community to ensure all students experience belonging and reach their potential.
Target Communities: Seattle Public Schools students, particularly those furthest from educational justice; focus on Black/African American male students; Title I school communities
Additional Themes: Food security, basic needs, teacher diversity, racial equity, educational justice, school leadership development

Mission/Summary: ACRS is a social justice organization that promotes the health and well-being of Asians and Native Hawaiians/Pacific Islanders and other communities by providing and advocating for responsive community-based services.
Target Communities: Asian Americans, Native Hawaiians/Pacific Islanders, and other underserved communities
Address: 3639 Martin Luther King Jr. Way S, Seattle, WA 98144
Additional Themes: Food security, immigrant rights, civic engagement, workforce development, behavioral health, problem gambling support

Mission/Summary: Atlantic Street Center supports families and communities in raising healthy, successful children and youth through direct services and advocacy for social justice and equity. They provide wrap-around services including early education, family support, and behavioral health services to families in need.
Target Communities: Families with children and youth in King County, Seattle, and North Pierce County; emphasis on underserved populations and families in need
Geographic Area: King County, Seattle, and North Pierce County
Additional Themes: Youth development, early childhood education, family support, behavioral health, gender-based violence prevention, social justice advocacy

Mission/Summary: Bike Works promotes the bicycle as a vehicle for change to empower youth and build resilient communities. The organization has operated since 1996, focusing on educating and empowering youth while making bicycling accessible and affordable to Seattle residents.
Target Communities: Youth ages 9-17 and adults 18+; Seattle community members seeking affordable bicycle access and education
Address: 3715 S Hudson St, Suite #111 (Lower Level), Seattle, WA 98118
Additional Themes: Environmental sustainability, bicycle repair education, affordable bicycle services, youth mechanics education

Mission/Summary: BESR is a Washington-based organization focused on advancing educational equity for Black students. The organization works toward closing the opportunity gap through strategic initiatives and collaborative efforts with state agencies.
Target Communities: Black students and families in Washington State; focus on educational equity and opportunity gaps
Address: 33530 1st Way S. Suite 102, Federal Way, WA 98003
Additional Themes: Educational equity, policy advocacy, legislative action, opportunity gap reduction

Mission/Summary: Bureau of Fearless Ideas is part of the 826 National network, providing free writing and tutoring programs for students. The organization uses creative writing to help young people develop literacy skills and confidence.
Target Communities: Students ages 6-18 in Seattle
Additional Themes: Literacy development, creative writing, tutoring, volunteer-based programming

Mission/Summary: The Center for Ethical Leadership cultivates leadership and builds capacity for change, helping communities tap collective wisdom in service of the common good. The organization focuses on ethical leadership development, creating inclusive spaces for dialogue, and advancing social change across sectors.
Target Communities: Diverse populations historically excluded from leadership spaces; focus on race, ethnicity, age, and socioeconomic differences
Additional Themes: Youth development, civic engagement, social justice, human rights, education, peacemaking circles, trauma-informed leadership

Mission/Summary: C.H.A.M.P.S (Changing Habits And Motivating Personal Self-esteem) is a Seattle-based nonprofit focused on community engagement and resident support. The organization operates through three core areas: Housing & Financial Stability, Public Health & Safety, and Community Participation.
Target Communities: Southeast Seattle youth and families; Rainier Beach residents; underserved community members needing housing, healthcare, and financial stability support
Geographic Area: Rainier Beach area of Southeast Seattle
Additional Themes: Housing and financial stability, public health and safety, transitional housing, resource referral, community events

Mission/Summary: CISC helps immigrants throughout King County achieve success in their new community by providing information, referral, advocacy, social, and support services. The organization focuses on bridging cultures and creating opportunities for immigrant families.
Target Communities: Immigrant families, youth, seniors, and disabled adults throughout King County
Additional Themes: Healthcare access, early childhood education, senior care services, hate/bias response, cultural preservation, immigration services

Mission/Summary: City Fruit promotes and preserves urban fruit to nourish people, build community, and protect the environment. Since 2008, they have stewarded and harvested fruit from public and private trees across Seattle to support a more sustainable and just food system.
Target Communities: Food-insecure populations in Seattle; schools; food banks; meals programs; community partners
Address: 4000 Aurora Ave N Suite 123, Seattle, WA 98103
Additional Themes: Food security, equitable food access, urban agriculture, orchard stewardship, community fruit tree care, Coast Salish traditional foodways

Organization Name: City of Seattle, Department of Neighborhoods
Mission/Summary: Strengthens Seattle by actively engaging all communities through providing resources and opportunities for community members to build strong communities and improve their quality of life. Works through community engagement coordinators and neighborhood councils.
Target Communities: Neighborhoods and residents citywide; community-based organizations and neighborhood councils across seven City Council Districts
Additional Themes: P-Patch community gardening, historic preservation, neighborhood matching fund grants, food equity fund, community storytelling, civic participation

Organization Name: City of Seattle, Office for Civil Rights
Mission/Summary: SOCR works to advance civil rights and end barriers to equity by enforcing anti-discrimination laws in employment, housing, public accommodations, and contracting within Seattle city limits. Focuses on policy priorities and institutional change through the Race and Social Justice Initiative.
Target Communities: Communities of color, LGBTQ individuals, renters, people with disabilities, and underserved populations experiencing discrimination
Additional Themes: Housing discrimination, fair housing rights, criminal history protections for renters, gender justice, LGBTQ visibility initiatives, Youth Civic Engagement Program (YCEP), participatory budgeting

Organization Name: City of Seattle, Department of Education and Early Learning
Mission/Summary: DEEL administers early learning programs and educational initiatives for Seattle families, including preschool, child care assistance, and community partnership programs. Provides youth mental health resources and school-based health centers through the FEPP Levy.
Target Communities: Families with children ages 0-12+ years; childcare providers; preschool-age children (3-4 years); low-income families eligible for assistance programs
Additional Themes: Early learning, preschool, child care assistance, summer learning programs, youth telehealth services, quality practice & professional development, wraparound services, culturally specific investments, prenatal-to-three community grants

Mission/Summary: City Year AmeriCorps members devote themselves to changing the education status in Seattle and creating environments where young people feel like they belong so they can fulfill their potential. Partners with educators and challenges the educational status quo.
Target Communities: Seattle Public Schools students; Greater Seattle area; King County
Additional Themes: Youth development through AmeriCorps service model, education opportunity advocacy

Mission/Summary: Inspires students to be their best selves through mentoring, learning experiences, and community building. Goes beyond traditional one-to-one mentoring by creating multiple levels of support and connection for program participants.
Target Communities: Seattle students and adult mentors
Address: 1200 12th Ave S, Suite 710, Mailbox #14, Seattle, WA 98144
Additional Themes: Youth development, critical mentoring, Learning Community and Duos programs

Organization Name: Communities In Schools of Greater King County
Mission/Summary: Surrounds students with a community of support, empowering them to stay in school and achieve in life. Addresses opportunity gaps in education through an integrated student support model working directly in schools across multiple districts.
Target Communities: Students furthest from educational justice across 39 schools in Renton, Tukwila, Lake Washington, Issaquah, and Seattle school districts; addresses race, English proficiency, income disparities
Geographic Area: Greater King County region (Renton, Tukwila, Lake Washington, Issaquah, Seattle)
Additional Themes: Attendance improvement, behavioral support, academic coursework support, socioemotional learning, case management

Mission/Summary: Community-based 501(C)3 non-profit organization established in 1989 to create a support network and serve as the hub of services for people of Congolese descent. Helps immigrants integrate into American society through various support services.
Target Communities: Congolese immigrants and people of Congolese descent in Washington State and neighboring states
Additional Themes: Immigration assistance, job readiness/resume writing, interpreter and document translation services, local business support, cultural programming, community health board

Mission/Summary: Empowers families in Seattle. Serves East African communities with services in Somali, Amharic, and Arabic languages. (Limited information available on website)
Target Communities: East African families in Seattle (Somali, Amharic, and Arabic-speaking communities)
Additional Themes: Translation services in Somali, Amharic, and Arabic

Mission/Summary: El Centro de la Raza is a voice and a hub for the Latino/a community in King County. Founded in 1972, they provide 43 programs addressing poverty, racism, and systemic inequality through collective action and community empowerment.
Target Communities: Latino/a communities; serves all demographics; focuses on underserved populations experiencing poverty and systemic barriers
Geographic Area: Seattle (Beacon Hill), Federal Way, and King County; expanding early learning centers to Roosevelt, Columbia City, International District, and Northgate (2026)
Address: Seattle: 2524 16th Ave S, Seattle, WA 98144; Federal Way: 1607 S 341st PL, Federal Way, WA 98003
Additional Themes: Housing (affordable housing), food security (food bank - 312,403 lbs distributed in 2024), workforce development, job training, small business services, financial empowerment, credit building, early learning (ages 15 months-5 years), youth programs (ages 11-24), recreation (skate rink)

Mission/Summary: The only statewide civil rights organization focused on revolutionizing education so that a child's race and zip code aren't the predicating factors in defining their success. Works to eliminate opportunity gaps and promote success from birth through career by addressing policy, funding, and digital equity.
Target Communities: Communities of color throughout Washington state, with emphasis on Black and Brown communities; ConnectWA Coalition reaches 51,000+ residents
Additional Themes: Digital equity and technology access, digital navigation services (210,000+ services since 2022), TechConnectWA help desk, food security (distributed $5 million in food and rental assistance during COVID-19), school funding advocacy, research and data on systemic inequities, translation services in 15+ languages

Mission/Summary: Equity Matters is a Seattle-based women of color consulting team specializing in racial justice transformation. They support organizations through collaborative planning, assessments, workshops, and design labs to create systemic racial equity change across government, educational, and nonprofit sectors.
Target Communities: Organizations across multiple sectors (government entities, educational institutions, philanthropic and nonprofit organizations) seeking racial equity transformation
Additional Themes: Racial equity consulting and organizational transformation, professional development, strategic planning, inclusive hiring, community engagement tools

Mission/Summary: The Ethiopian Community in Seattle launched in 1980 to help the community gain a foothold in Seattle and the surrounding area. ECS provides culturally appropriate social services and programs for more than 5,000 members of the Ethiopian community in King County, helping overcome cultural and racial barriers to promote a thriving, self-sustaining community.
Target Communities: Ethiopian immigrants and East African communities in King County; serves more than 5,000 community members
Address: 8323 Rainier Ave S, Suite A, Seattle, WA 98118
Additional Themes: Immigration services, health services, housing assistance, legal services, employment support, small business development, cultural education and preservation, social services, community organizing

Mission/Summary: The foundation introduces young people to rowing since 1984, helping them find, start and stay rowing through programs designed to create opportunities for youth who need them most. They serve 55,000+ young people annually through school-based programs and partnerships with Pacific Northwest boathouses.
Target Communities: Young people in Pacific Northwest, particularly underserved populations; operates ERG ED® school-based programs and works with 50+ partners and affiliates
Additional Themes: Youth athletic development through rowing, financial aid and scholarships ($500K+ annually), uniforms, transportation, coaching support, Camp Lucy, middle school and junior rowing programs

Mission/Summary: The Indian Parent Advisory Committee (IPAC) advises Seattle Public Schools on the Native Education Title VI Indian Education Grant. The IPAC participates in approving grant applications, budgets, and amendments while representing Native American stakeholders who benefit from Title VI programming.
Target Communities: Native American/Alaska Native students enrolled in Seattle Public Schools who have completed voluntary 506 identification forms; works with Muckleshoot and Suquamish tribes
Address: John Stanford Center for Educational Excellence, 2445 3rd Ave. S, Seattle, WA 98134
Additional Themes: Cultural programming including Since Time Immemorial curriculum (state-mandated tribal history and culture), credit retrieval support, government-to-government tribal partnerships

Mission/Summary: Kandelia works to remove barriers to immigrant and refugee success in Seattle. They provide comprehensive support services for immigrant and refugee communities.
Target Communities: Immigrants and refugees in Seattle
Address: Seattle, WA 98118 (full street address not available)
Additional Themes: Immigration and refugee support services, Roots and Routes: Immigrant Stories in Seattle series

Mission/Summary: Launch is a Seattle-based nonprofit founded in 1977 that provides effective, affordable early learning, before- & after-school enrichment, early preschool, and summer expanded learning opportunity programs. They serve more than 800 children ages 2-12 years old annually during foundational years when their social-emotional and academic support help launch young lives on a trajectory of success.
Target Communities: Children ages 2-12 years old in Seattle; families seeking affordable early learning and enrichment programs
Additional Themes: Early childhood education (ages 0-8), before and after-school programs, summer programs, early preschool, social-emotional development, values include kindness, compassion, continuous improvement

Mission/Summary: League of Education Voters champions an equitable public education system across Washington state, working with families, educators, and leaders to build student success from early learning through higher education and career readiness. They focus on advocacy, policy research, and building capacity for education coalitions.
Target Communities: Students across Washington state with particular emphasis on communities of color and equity; South Shore PreK-8 in South Seattle serves as a practical model
Geographic Area: Statewide Washington coverage with South Seattle programs referenced
Email: Available via contact form at educationvoters.org/contact/ (staff format: firstname@educationvoters.org)
Additional Themes: Youth advocacy, policy research, legislative advocacy, higher education access, early childhood development, student homelessness, special education funding, racial equity in education systems, school discipline transformation, universal pre-K, safe and inclusive school environments

Mission/Summary: Mercy Housing Northwest owns and operates 52 properties throughout Washington, Oregon, and Idaho, providing over 5,000 families and seniors with an affordable place to call home. The organization combines permanent affordable housing with onsite resident services focused on stability, health, education, and financial well-being.
Target Communities: Low-income families, seniors, individuals with developmental disabilities, homeless/at-risk populations, veterans, educators across 22 counties
Geographic Area: Washington, Oregon, and Idaho (22 counties); Northwest regional office in Seattle
Address: 6930 Martin Luther King Jr. Way S, Seattle, WA 98118
Additional Themes: Affordable housing development, homeownership assistance (100+ families), after-school tutoring and homework club, ESL coaching, nutrition workshops, health education, employment coaching, tax preparation, emergency assistance, case management, eviction prevention, health and housing integration

Mission/Summary: Minds Matter Seattle connects high-achieving students from low-income families with mentorship, test preparation, and college guidance to help them attend their college of choice and create futures of impact. The organization provides summer programs and college preparation programming.
Target Communities: High-achieving high school students from low-income families and neighborhoods in the Seattle area
Additional Themes: College preparation and guidance, volunteer mentorship, student leadership development, college access for underrepresented populations, business-focused summer programs at universities

Mission/Summary: MomsRising is a national network advocating for family-friendly policies and systemic change. They mobilize moms and supporters around issues including workplace justice, maternal health, early care and education, and reproductive rights to transform conditions for families nationwide.
Target Communities: Moms and people who love them nationwide, with particular attention to marginalized communities (Black women, immigrant families, low-income families); operates across United States with state-level organizing
Geographic Area: National (United States) - operates across federal and state levels including Washington state
Additional Themes: Gun control advocacy, immigration/immigrant family rights, federal budget priorities for families, voter suppression prevention, reproductive rights, workplace justice, maternal health, early care and education, Moms Next Door program, State Moms Forces, dismantling school-to-prison pipeline

Mission/Summary: Founded in 1906, Neighborhood House is one of the oldest human services agencies in the Seattle region, providing comprehensive programs addressing the needs of entire families, particularly low-income community members, public housing residents, immigrants, and refugees.
Target Communities: Low-income community members, public housing residents, immigrants, refugees, and entire families
Geographic Area: Seattle region and King County area
Address: 1225 South Weller Street, Suite 510, Seattle, WA 98144
Additional Themes: Housing stability, aging and disability services, employment and adult education, early childhood education, youth development, community health (services in 40+ languages)

Mission/Summary: OneAmerica organizes immigrant and refugee leaders to build power in communities and run campaigns for a just immigration system, inclusive education for all, and a truly representative democracy.
Target Communities: Immigrant and refugee families and workers across Washington State
Additional Themes: Immigration policy and advocacy, civic engagement, voting power, democracy reform, workforce development, economic inclusion, English classes, citizenship support

Mission/Summary: Information not accessible from website. Organization appears to focus on youth education and global awareness programs based on organization name and social media presence.
Target Communities: Unknown
Additional Themes: Website inaccessible for detailed information. Social media: Facebook @oneworldnow, Twitter @oneworldnow

Mission/Summary: RBAC is a Black-led grassroots organization dedicated to locally driven development in Seattle's Rainier Beach neighborhood. They promote quality education, living wage jobs, affordable housing and transportation, and build community capacity to create a connected, sustainable, equitable community.
Target Communities: Rainier Beach neighborhood residents, with focus on youth and Black homeowners in South Seattle, South King, and North Pierce Counties
Geographic Area: Rainier Beach neighborhood, South Seattle, South King County
Address: 9013 Martin Luther King Jr Way S, Seattle, WA 98118
Additional Themes: Food justice, housing initiatives, economic development, transportation planning, environmental sustainability, parks/gardens/farms development, Back2School Bash, youth journalism

Mission/Summary: Parent Teacher Student Association supporting Rainier Beach High School community. Specific PTSA programming details not available from school website.
Target Communities: Rainier Beach High School students and families
Geographic Area: Rainier Beach neighborhood in South Seattle
Address: 8815 Seward Park Ave. S, Seattle, WA 98118
Additional Themes: School support and family engagement

Mission/Summary: Rainier Scholars offers a 12-year pathway to college graduation for low-income students of color, beginning in 5th grade and extending through college completion. The organization cultivates academic and leadership potential through rigorous, transformative opportunities.
Target Communities: Low-income students of color in Seattle and Tacoma; approximately 100 new scholars join annually, with specific focus on African American students in Tacoma program
Additional Themes: College access and completion, leadership development, critical thinking, resilience building, summer programs, mental health resources, career development

Mission/Summary: RVC Seattle provides fiscal sponsorship, capacity building, and operations support to BIPOC grassroots organizations in Seattle-King County. They help community-based organizations develop sustainable infrastructure while maintaining their focus on equity and social justice work.
Target Communities: BIPOC (Black, Indigenous, People of Color) grassroots organizations and leaders of color in Seattle-King County
Address: 3715 S Hudson St, Suite 103, Seattle, WA 98118
Additional Themes: Organizational sustainability, nonprofit infrastructure development, racial equity, social justice, Community Impact Fellowship with leadership training and mentorship

Mission/Summary: Reading Partners delivers evidence-based, individualized literacy tutoring to elementary students in Seattle. They provide twice-weekly tutoring grounded in the science of reading, partnering with community organizations to expand reach beyond direct programming.
Target Communities: K-2 students in underserved communities; works through schools and community-based organizations
Additional Themes: Literacy development, educational equity, science of reading instruction, individualized tutoring, high-dosage intervention, family literacy resources. 2024-25: 91% of K-2 students developing mastery, 329 students served, 9,852 tutoring sessions

Mission/Summary: ReWA provides refugees and immigrants in Puget Sound with wraparound social services to thrive. The organization delivers eight integrated services in over 65 languages, supporting families across employment, housing, legal, educational, and health domains.
Target Communities: Refugees and immigrants in the Puget Sound region, with particular focus on women and families from diverse backgrounds
Address: 4008 Martin Luther King, Jr. Way South, Seattle, WA 98108
Additional Themes: Housing and homelessness prevention, employment and vocational training, early childhood development, domestic violence support, immigration legal services, naturalization, citizenship preparation, behavioral health, gender-based violence survivor support

Mission/Summary: Sawhorse Revolution fosters confident, community-oriented youth through the power of carpentry and craft. The organization teams high school students with professional carpenters and architects to design and build community projects addressing local needs.
Target Communities: High school youth in the Seattle area; projects serve homeless populations and broader community members
Additional Themes: Housing (tiny homes for homeless), library construction, garden infrastructure, youth workforce development, Indigenous community prioritization, carpentry and construction skills training, design thinking

Mission/Summary: Information not accessible from website due to technical limitations. Organization serves Washington state.
Additional Themes: Website technical issues prevented data extraction

Mission/Summary: Faith-based organization serving the Chinese community in Seattle through Christian ministry and worship across multiple language groups (Cantonese, English, and Mandarin).
Target Communities: Chinese immigrant and ethnic Chinese communities in Seattle; organized by language preference (Cantonese, English, Mandarin speakers) and age groups (youth, children)
Additional Themes: Religious education, spiritual formation, pastoral care, multilingual ministry services, youth ministries, children ministries

Mission/Summary: Three-college system offering workforce education, career training, and academic transfer programs. Provides certificate and degree programs across multiple specialty centers while serving diverse student populations, including high school students through Running Start and Seattle residents through the Seattle Promise initiative.
Target Communities: High school students, working adults, workforce retraining participants, international students, Seattle public school graduates (through Seattle Promise)
Additional Themes: Workforce development, career training certificates (130+ programs), tuition-free college access initiatives, maritime education, wood technology training, healthcare education

Mission/Summary: Represents over 80 PTAs/PTSAs in Seattle Public Schools, centering voices of those furthest from educational justice. Supports, informs, and advocates for school communities citywide with mission to make every child's potential a reality by engaging and empowering families.
Target Communities: Seattle Public Schools families and students; emphasis on communities furthest from educational justice
Additional Themes: Grant funding to local PTAs, Diversity Equity & Inclusion focus, mental health resources, safety and reporting resources, support for LGBTQIA+ immigrant and disability communities, Reflections program (student art competition), School of Excellence program

Organization Name: Seattle Public Schools School and Community Partnerships
Mission/Summary: Builds collaborative, cross-sector partnership systems facilitating consistent and student-centered ecosystem of supports for students, with vision of transformational, equity-driven, anti-racist partnerships.
Target Communities: Seattle Public Schools students and families across all grade levels; emphasis on low-income students, students of color, and those furthest from educational justice
Address: John Stanford Center for Educational Excellence, 2445 3rd Ave. S, Seattle, WA 98134

Mission/Summary: Facilitates human and school transformation through mindfulness practices. Works to ensure all students are well and whole by providing mindfulness-based social emotional learning as a life skill in the Seattle area and beyond.
Target Communities: Students, teachers, parents, and school communities in the Seattle area; serves diverse student populations including elementary through high school students
Address: Brain and Body Recess, 3820 S Ferdinand St #201A, Seattle, WA 98118
Additional Themes: Youth development, mental health promotion, racial equity, professional development for educators, systems transformation through mindfulness practice, emotion management, self-awareness, stress/anxiety reduction

Mission/Summary: Closes the opportunity gap for women and BIPOC communities in STEM education and careers. Offers six programs serving students from kindergarten through high school, focusing on creating equity in STEM across King County.
Target Communities: Women and BIPOC students (K-12); focus on South King County's diverse communities and underrepresented populations in STEM fields
Geographic Area: King County with primary focus on South King County; serves multiple school districts across the region
Address: 3250 Airport Way S Suite 742, Seattle, WA 98134
Additional Themes: Youth leadership development, mentorship programs, workforce training, career pathway development in STEM fields, hands-on projects in coding, 3D design, biomimicry, food science, makerspace (The Lab)

Mission/Summary: Mission is to assist children, youth and families in King County in achieving safe, stable and sustainable lives. Black-led nonprofit serving South Seattle for over 50 years providing culturally relevant mental health and family support services.
Target Communities: Children, youth, and families in South Seattle and King County, with emphasis on underserved and diverse communities
Geographic Area: South Seattle and greater King County
Additional Themes: Youth violence prevention, wraparound family services, early childhood development (ParentChild+), teen mental health support, individual/family/couples therapy, support groups for teens and parents, multilingual staff

Mission/Summary: Works to close education gaps in public schools by combining technology with engaged coaches to help students achieve measurable improvements in math proficiency. Students double their academic growth rates, with all cohorts significantly outperforming peers not enrolled in the program.
Target Communities: Public school students in Washington state, particularly those not meeting basic math standards. Four school partners: Stevenson, TOPS, Lake Hills, and Baldwin
Additional Themes: Youth development through academic coaching, building student confidence and motivation, personalized game-based learning approaches, after-school tutoring model, data-driven systems change

Mission/Summary: Provides free access to books, digital resources, online learning tools, and community programs across 27 locations in Seattle. Serves as a public institution offering cultural programming, research materials, and educational support to all residents.
Target Communities: All Seattle residents; specific focus on families with young children, teens, seniors (Adults 50+), and multilingual communities
Additional Themes: Digital literacy, e-books/audiobooks access, genealogy research, interlibrary loans, unique collections, world languages collection, free public computer/internet access, story times, arts & culture events, author talks, STEM resources, civics & social services programming, free tax help

Mission/Summary: Urban Impact works to break the cycle of generational poverty and create communities where everyone is engaged and thriving. They focus on building healthy relationships and promoting education, economic opportunity, safety, health, racial justice, and spiritual foundation.
Target Communities: Primarily North Seattle neighborhoods, serving families experiencing poverty, youth, and underserved communities
Additional Themes: Economic development, entrepreneurship support (Shark's Pitch Accelerator), health and wellness, spiritual/faith-based community engagement, neighborhood transformation, educational support

Organization Name: University of Washington College of Education - Danforth Educational Leadership Program
Mission/Summary: The program prepares equity-driven educators to become outstanding principals and program administrators in P–12 school systems through innovative, competency-based training combining classroom instruction, cutting-edge research, and intensive internship experiences.
Target Communities: Educators seeking principal/administrator certification; current school-based instructional staff; emphasis on serving equity-driven leaders working in P–12 schools and districts
Geographic Area: Seattle-based program with primary focus on Washington state; preference for Seattle Public Schools internship placements
Address: 2012 Skagit Lane, Miller Hall, Box 353600, Seattle, WA 98195-3600
Additional Themes: Educational leadership development; culturally responsive leadership practices; ethical leadership; instructional capacity building; data-driven decision making; 100% graduation completion rate with 96% employment placement

Organization Name: University of Washington CELE Center - Community Engagement & Leadership Education
Mission/Summary: The CELE Center provides students with opportunities to develop the knowledge, skills and attitudes needed to contribute to thriving communities through authentic community and campus partnerships. Programs include K-12 tutoring and mentoring, community-engaged courses, leadership development, and the Othello-UW Commons serving Southeast Seattle.
Target Communities: UW students, K-12 students in partner schools, Southeast Seattle communities (Othello area), schools throughout Seattle and Washington
Geographic Area: Seattle and broader Washington state, with specific focus on Southeast Seattle through Othello-UW Commons
Address: 171 Mary Gates Hall, Seattle, WA 98195, Box 352803
Additional Themes: Youth development, leadership education, civic engagement, community-engaged learning, educational partnerships (Riverways Education Partnerships), tutoring programs, community organizing

Mission/Summary: WA-BLOC partners with Seattle public school communities to create culturally responsive, trauma-informed, restorative education. They design and facilitate programming to nurture restorative practices, student voice, and cultural empowerment for Black and brown students in southeast Seattle.
Target Communities: Black and brown scholars in southeast Seattle; K-8th grade students; school staff and families in Seattle Public Schools
Geographic Area: Southeast Seattle, with specific partnerships at John Muir, Lowell, Wing Luke, Emerson Elementary Schools
Address: 3715 S Hudson St, Suite 103, Seattle, WA 98118
Additional Themes: Youth leadership development, social justice advocacy, intergenerational organizing, equity in education, restorative justice practices, Freedom Schools summer program, BLOC Organizing

Mission/Summary: City Fruit promotes and preserves urban fruit to nourish people, build community, and protect the environment. Since 2008, they have stewarded and harvested fruit from public and private trees across Seattle to support a more sustainable and equitable food system.
Target Communities: Schools, food banks, and meals programs across Seattle; focus on equitable access to healthy, local foods for underserved populations
Geographic Area: Seattle, Washington (broader city-wide focus with community partners throughout the area)
Address: 4000 Aurora Ave N Suite 123, Seattle, WA 98103
Additional Themes: Food security and access (primary focus), urban agriculture, environmental sustainability, community building, orchard stewardship, fruit tree care services, educational workshops

Mission/Summary: Woodland Park Zoo saves wildlife and inspires conservation action. They engage visitors through educational experiences while working to protect animals and their habitats globally.
Target Communities: General public visitors; families and children; multilingual communities (Spanish, Russian, Mandarin Chinese, Vietnamese, and Somali)
Additional Themes: Wildlife conservation, environmental education, zoo-based learning experiences, field trips, summer camps, Community Access Program for equitable access, conservation stewardship education

Mission/Summary: Y-WE is a nonprofit offering mentorship-based programs to young women and gender expansive youth ages 13-26 within a collaborative community focused on leadership development and wellness services.
Target Communities: Young women, trans, non-binary, and gender-expansive youth ages 13-26 in Seattle
Geographic Area: Seattle, Washington, with focus on South Seattle (Rainier Valley area)
Additional Themes: Youth empowerment, mentorship, leadership development, community belonging, social change advocacy, healing justice, wellness services
